expectations.text: Proceeded because inputs (audience, goal, timeline) are rich
expectations.evidence: The response proceeds directly to the campaign brief with only a brief assumption note ('Assuming this is a SaaS product with a standard free-trial signup flow'), without asking gating questions.

The Objective section states 'Drive free-trial signups from freelance designers over a 6-week period. (Set a specific numeric target based on your current baseline — e.g., 300 net new trials.)' — it defers the specific number to the user rather than committing to one.

expectations.text: Articulates the audience before->after belief shift
expectations.evidence: The Audience section states 'Current belief: Tracking time is more hassle than it's worth...' and 'Desired belief after the campaign: This tool fits the way designers actually work...'

expectations.text: States a single key message plus a reason to believe
expectations.evidence: The brief states a Single Key Message: 'Stop guessing how long projects take. Know exactly what to charge.' and includes a 'Reason to Believe' section with proof points.

expectations.text: Includes CTA, channels, timeline, and success metrics
expectations.evidence: The brief includes 'Call to Action: Start your free trial — no credit card needed', a Channels table, a Timeline table by week, and a Success Metrics section.
